name: Spot by NetApp Ocean Kubernetes API
what_it_does: Kubernetes-aware cloud cost optimization platform with REST API — enabling DevOps and FinOps teams to automatically provision and scale Kubernetes nodes using spot/preemptible instances (AWS Spot, Azure Spot, GCP Preemptible) with intelligent instance diversification, automatic workload rescheduling on interruption, and significant cost savings (60-90% reduction).
use_cases: Optimizing Kubernetes node costs via Ocean API for automatic spot instance provisioning
Managing Ocean clusters via API for infrastructure-as-code spot instance configuration
Analyzing cost savings via Ocean API for FinOps reporting and optimization insights
Configuring virtual node groups via Ocean API for workload-specific instance selection
Integrating Ocean with CI/CD via API for automated cluster scaling configuration
not_for: Non-Kubernetes workloads (use Spot Elastigroup for EC2/VM-level spot optimization)
Workloads requiring guaranteed on-demand instances (spot interruptions are unavoidable)
Teams not on AWS, Azure, or GCP (Spot Ocean is cloud-specific)
best_when: A DevOps or FinOps team running Kubernetes on AWS/Azure/GCP wants to dramatically reduce node costs using spot instances, with intelligent handling of spot interruptions and automatic workload rescheduling.
avoid_when: You have stateful workloads that cannot tolerate spot interruptions, need on-premises Kubernetes, or run a different container orchestrator.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication. Authorization: Bearer {api_token} header. API token from Spot console (spot.io). REST API at api.spotinst.io/ocean/aws/k8s/cluster/ (AWS) or /azure/k8s/ (Azure) or /gcp/k8s/ (GCP). Account ID required in all requests (X-Account-ID header or query param). Terraform provider: spotinst/terraform-provider-spotinst. Python SDK: spotinst-sdk2. Webhooks for cost events and scale events.

pricing.paid_tiers: Percentage of savings model; typically 20-25% of savings generated
pricing.notes: US company (Tel Aviv origin). Spot.io (formerly Spotinst) acquired by NetApp in 2020 for ~$450M. NetApp subsidiary. Spot Ocean: Kubernetes-specific. Spot Elastigroup: VM/EC2-level (separate product). Ocean pricing: percentage of savings (pay-for-value model) or flat subscription. Savings: 60-90% reduction in Kubernetes compute costs vs on-demand. Instance diversification: automatically selects from multiple spot instance types to reduce interruption risk. Interruption handling: 2-minute notice handling, automatic rescheduling. Savings analysis: cost reporting and right-sizing recommendations. Integration: EKS, AKS, GKE, KOPS, Rancher.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token + X-Account-ID required. Terraform provider primary IaC path. Cloud-specific endpoints (AWS/Azure/GCP). NetApp subsidiary (Spotinst). Pay-for-savings pricing model. Webhooks. Python/Go SDKs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cluster reads idempotent by cluster ID. Configuration updates: idempotent by cluster ID (PUT replaces). Cost data reads: idempotent by date range.
known_agent_gotchas: X-Account-ID header required on all requests — account ID from Spot console, not cloud account ID
Ocean (Kubernetes) vs Elastigroup (VM/EC2) are different APIs — ensure correct product selection
Cloud-specific endpoints: separate API paths for AWS (/ocean/aws/k8s/), Azure (/ocean/azure/k8s/), GCP
Spot interruptions are inevitable: design Kubernetes workloads with PodDisruptionBudgets for graceful handling
Terraform provider is the recommended IaC path — REST API for automation beyond Terraform
Savings pricing model: 20-25% of savings — validate savings calculation methodology before contract

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token. SOC2 certified. NetApp parent company: enterprise security standards. Spot Ocean manages cloud infrastructure credentials (AWS IAM role, Azure SP, GCP SA) — high-value access. Cloud provider IAM roles must use least-privilege. X-Account-ID separation: account isolation. Webhooks: verify webhook signatures. US data residency — GDPR compliance via DPA.
